title: Entrepreneurship Basics
subtitle: ideas into action!
overview: This session introduces students to the fundamentals of entrepreneurship, innovation, problem-solving, and how businesses are created from ideas. Students will learn entrepreneurial thinking, teamwork, creativity, and confidence-building skills.

images._note: Add photos from entrepreneurship activities, brainstorming sessions, startup examples, business model drawings, and student presentations.

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Photo of students brainstorming startup ideas
gallery.caption: Every business starts with an IDEA!

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Students working together in teams
gallery.caption: Entrepreneurs work together to solve problems

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Student presenting a business idea
gallery.caption: Confidence and communication are key skills

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Business model or planning worksheet photo
gallery.caption: Planning helps ideas become successful businesses
Pitch your idea like a real entrepreneur!

keyTopics: What is Entrepreneurship? — Turning Ideas into Businesses
Problem Solving — Entrepreneurs solve everyday problems
Risk Taking — Trying new ideas even when uncertain
Leadership — Inspiring and guiding a team
Business Basics — Product, Price, Promotion, Profit
Startup Pitch — Explaining your idea confidently

activity.title: Create Your Own Startup Idea
steps: Identify a real-world problem students face
Choose the best idea as a team
Give your startup a name and logo
Present a 1-minute startup pitch to the class

options: A person who starts and runs a business
quiz.explanation: An entrepreneur is someone who creates and runs a business or startup.

options: Solve problems with ideas and innovation
quiz.explanation: Entrepreneurs identify problems and create solutions that help people.

options: Creating new ideas or improving old ones
quiz.explanation: Innovation means creating something new or improving existing ideas.

quiz.q: Why is teamwork important in entrepreneurship?
options: One person can do everything alone
quiz.explanation: Great businesses are often built by teams with different talents.

options: People who buy or use products/services
quiz.explanation: Customers are the people who use or purchase products and services.
A startup is a new business built around an idea or innovation.

quiz.q: Which skill helps entrepreneurs explain ideas confidently?
quiz.explanation: Communication helps entrepreneurs share and pitch their ideas effectively.
Entrepreneurs often take risks when trying new ideas and innovations.

quiz.q: Which of these is an example of entrepreneurship?
options: Creating a recycling app for schools
quiz.explanation: Entrepreneurship involves creating useful solutions and businesses.

quiz.q: Why is creativity important for entrepreneurs?
quiz.explanation: Creative thinking helps entrepreneurs build unique products and solutions.
A business pitch is a short explanation of an idea to others.

quiz.q: What should entrepreneurs understand before building products?
quiz.explanation: Successful entrepreneurs understand customer problems and needs first.

quiz.q: What does profit mean in business?
options: Giving away products for free forever
quiz.explanation: Profit is the money left after paying business costs.

quiz.q: Which quality is important for entrepreneurs?
quiz.explanation: Leadership helps entrepreneurs guide teams and grow businesses.

quiz.q: What is the first step in creating a business idea?
quiz.explanation: Most successful businesses start by solving a real problem.
